mcqs.question: What is the primary purpose of disabling interrupts in a processor?

options.text: To prevent the interruption of critical code sections
To speed up overall system performance
To allow the processor to enter a low-power state
To enable communication between different hardware devices

mcqs.explanation: Interrupts are disabled to protect critical sections of code that must execute atomically, without being preempted by an interrupt service routine, ensuring data consistency and system stability.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is a typical mechanism used by a processor to enable and disable interrupts?

options.text: A dedicated status bit in a special-purpose register
A specific memory-mapped I/O address
A separate control signal on the processor bus
A dedicated instruction that toggles an external pin

mcqs.explanation: Most processors have a special status register (e.g., the Processor Status Word or Flags Register) which contains an interrupt enable bit. Setting or clearing this bit globally enables or disables the interrupt system.

mcqs.question: What is a major risk of keeping interrupts disabled for an extended period?

options.text: Missing time-sensitive interrupts and degrading system responsiveness
Corrupting the data in the processor's general-purpose registers
Causing a permanent hardware failure in the interrupt controller
Preventing the processor from fetching the next instruction

mcqs.explanation: If interrupts are disabled for too long, the system may fail to respond to critical, time-sensitive events (like a watchdog timer or real-time data arrival), which can lead to data loss or system failure.

mcqs.question: In a typical assembly language sequence for a critical section, what is the purpose of the EI instruction?

options.text: To re-enable interrupts after the critical section
To execute an interrupt service routine
To initialize the interrupt vector table
To disable higher priority interrupts only

mcqs.explanation: The EI (Enable Interrupts) instruction is used to re-enable the global interrupt system after a critical section protected by a preceding DI (Disable Interrupts) instruction has completed.

mcqs.question: What is the term for an interrupt that occurs while the processor is already executing an interrupt service routine?

options.text: Nested interrupt
Masked interrupt
Spurious interrupt
Vectored interrupt

mcqs.explanation: A nested interrupt occurs when a higher-priority interrupt preempts a currently executing lower-priority interrupt service routine. This requires careful management of interrupt enables.
